summary: Fluid guitars, soaring Scarlet>Fire and a dream second set
blurb: The Mosquee show delivers a tight first set highlighted by Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo and Jack Straw, then erupts into a legendary second‑set sandwich of Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain, Estimated Prophet → He's Gone → The Other One → Wharf Rat → The Wheel. Jerry’s tone is on fire, Phil’s bass drives the peaks, and Keith’s piano adds lush color throughout.
review:
May 25, 1977 at The Mosque in Richmond captured one of the spring ’77 high‑points. The first set rolls smoothly from a smoking Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo into a crisp Jack Straw, then eases through folk‑rock gems like Peggy‑O and a tasteful Lazy Lightning → Supplication pairing. The real magic ignites with the Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain segue – reviewers call it “soaring” and “otherworldly,” a perfect launchpad for an unforgettable Estimated Prophet → He's Gone → The Other One marathon that flows straight into Wharf Rat, then wraps up with a Wheel‑Around & Around sandwich. Jerry Garcia’s fluid guitar tone and incendiary solos dominate the night, while Phil Lesh’s punchy bass and Keith Godchaux’s bright piano keep the band grounded. The twin drummers lock in tight grooves that fuel the jams, and Bob Weir’s rhythm work and vocal contributions add steady momentum. Across all recordings the performance is described as a “dream set” with seamless transitions and high‑energy improvisation, making this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

The soundboard sources are uniformly excellent – the Shannon fix and Miller remasters are praised for crystal‑clear mixes, no drop‑outs, and perfect instrument separation. For those who prefer the raw live vibe, the audience tapes still capture the classic 1977 energy, especially a gorgeous Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain sequence. Overall, this night ranks among the band’s finest spring ’77 outings.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All eight detailed recordings are marked positive; reviewers repeatedly use superlatives like 'legendary', 'dream set', 'soaring' and highlight the same standout songs and seamless segues, indicating strong consensus of exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with fixed gap, crystal‑clear mix and no drop‑outs; consistently praised as the clearest representation of the performance.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain segue described as soaring and otherworldly
Estimated Prophet → He's Gone → The Other One marathon forms a dream second‑set climax
Jerry Garcia’s fluid guitar tone and incendiary solos throughout the night
Excellent soundboard recordings (Shannon fix and Miller remasters) provide pristine audio

band_performance.Jerry: Fluid, soaring guitar tone that borders on possessed; solos on Half‑Step, Estimated Prophet and The Other One are highlighted as incendiary.
band_performance.Phil: Punchy, commanding bass lines that fatten the mix, especially driving the peaks of The Other One and Scarlet Begonias.
band_performance.Bob: Steady rhythm work and vocal contributions that lift the band’s roller‑coaster energy, notably on Jack Straw and throughout the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Locked‑in drumming with tribal power; fuels transitions into The Other One and drives the second‑set marathon.
band_performance.Billy: Tight grooves and explosive fills that complement Mickey’s playing, especially during the Wheel → Around & Around sandwich.
